ga_description: This database uses Google Analytics, provided by Google, to understand access patterns. Google Analytics uses cookies to collect access information. The access information is collected anonymously and does not identify individuals. You can disable data collection by Google Analytics using the "Google Analytics Opt-out Add-on".
manual_data_1: This database is a retrieval system for the glyph list published in Georg Möller, Hieratische Paläographie, 3 volumes, 1909–12 (hereinafter referred to as "Hieratische Paläographie").
manual_data_2: As of January 31, 2021, it contains the main characters (1–613bis), numerals (614–719), and ligatures (I–LXXVII) from volumes 1–3.
manual_data_3_prefix: The original images of the Hieratische Paläographie are IIIF images provided by the Digital Library of the Asian Research Library, the University of Tokyo at
manual_data_4_prefix: IIIF image cropping and display uses
manual_data_4_suffix: provided by the Center for Open Data in the Humanities.
manual_lang: You can switch the display language (ENGLISH / Japanese).
manual_itemtype: You can select Main characters (1–613bis), Ligatures (I–LXXVII), and Numerals (614–719).
manual_subtype: As sub-categories of Numerals (614–719), you can select "Cardinal", "Date", "Fraction", "Length", "Area", and "Volume".
manual_unit: You can select "Single" and "Continuous". "Single" means the character is used independently. "Continuous" means two or more characters are written in sequence.
manual_itemlabel: Search by the item number listed in the Hieratische Paläographie (the number used as the heading of the data). Searching with an item number shows results including subdivisions. For example, searching for "192" shows "192, 192B, 192C".
manual_hieraticno: You can search by Hieratic number. Hieratic numbers generally match the item numbers. However, for some items, unique numbers have been assigned on this site through consolidation and splitting.
manual_categoryclass: You can narrow down by the classification class assigned to the hieroglyph number. The classification class refers to the alphabet at the beginning of the hieroglyph number. There are 26 types: A–Z (excluding J) and Aa.
manual_hieroglyphno: You can search for hieratic from hieroglyph numbers. The hieroglyph numbers used in this database follow the numbering below.
manual_hieroglyphno_note: However, some characters have been assigned unique auxiliary symbols. When searching by hieroglyph number, please enter "classification class (alphabet) + number" such as G17 or Aa2. Entering only the classification class (alphabet) will not return results.
manual_phoneword: You can search for hieratic by phonetic value or word. For searching, please enter alphabetic characters (half-width, English, uppercase, lowercase) using the Manuel de Codage (MdC) method. For example, to search for Ax, enter "Ax". Note that this database does not use auxiliary symbols for character arrangement (:)(*) or morpheme boundary symbols (.)(=). The Transliteration font is used for consonantal transliteration display.
manual_numeral: You can search for hieratic by numeral.
manual_vol: You can specify the volume number of the Hieratische Paläographie.
manual_page_desc: You can specify the page number of the main text of the Hieratische Paläographie. Only a specific single page can be specified.
manual_order: You can specify the arrangement order of character types within a page of the Hieratische Paläographie.
manual_note: You can search information entered in the notes.

concordance_header_link_note: Column headers with this icon link to the external database for that numbering system.

concordance_db_tsl_desc: Open digital sign repertoire linking Gardiner, Hieroglyphica, JSesh, and Unicode
